root_text.iti46:2.2: Sikkhānisaṁsānaṁ, bhikkhave, viharataṁ paññuttarānaṁ vimuttisārānaṁ satādhipateyyānaṁ dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.iti46:2.3: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā”ti.
root_text.iti46:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti46:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti46:2.1: “Mendicants, live with training as benefit, with wisdom as overseer, with freedom as core, and with mindfulness as ruler.
translation_text.iti46:2.2: A mendicant who lives in this way can expect one of two results:
translation_text.iti46:2.3: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.”
translation_text.iti46:3.1: “The training fulfilled, not liable to decline,
translation_text.iti46:3.2: overseen by wisdom, seer of rebirth’s end;
translation_text.iti46:3.3: that sage bears their final body;
translation_text.iti46:3.4: they have disposed of Māra, I declare, <j>and gone beyond old age.
translation_text.iti46:4.1: Therefore be always enjoying absorption, <j>immersed in samādhi,
translation_text.iti46:4.3: Having overcome Māra and his armies, mendicants,
translation_text.iti46:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
